title: veronica serpyllifolia subsp. humifusa
intro: Veronica serpyllifolia subsp. humifusa, also known as the prostrate speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is a low-growing, spreading herb with blue or purple flowers, often used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues. The medicinal properties of Veronica serpyllifolia subsp. humifusa include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can be used to treat conditions such as digestive problems and respiratory infections. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve making teas, infusions, or tinctures from its leaves and flowers, which can provide health benefits including reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation.
